title: Use a broad phase for collision
description: Cull candidate collision pairs with a spatial structure before doing exact narrow-phase tests, never checking every pair.
body: Use when many entities can collide. Do not test all n^2 pairs each frame, and do not run expensive narrow-phase math on objects that are nowhere near each other. Actions: maintain a spatial partition (uniform grid, quadtree/octree, or sweep-and-prune), query it to get only nearby candidate pairs (broad phase), then run precise shape tests (narrow phase) on those few. Validation signals include collision cost scaling far below n^2 as entity count grows and stable frame time with many objects. Risk boundary: O(n^2) collision silently caps the entity count and tanks frame rate at scale; a brute-force pair loop is a scalability defect once entity counts grow.
